description: Oracle test facts for stoichiometric conservation laws in chemical reaction networks. These come from the left null space of the stoichiometry matrix.

facts.context: For a chemical reaction network with stoichiometry matrix S, conservation laws come from:
facts.truth: the left null space of S (vectors w where w^T S = 0)

facts.context: For a reversible reaction A <-> B, the conserved quantity is:
In Michaelis-Menten enzyme kinetics E + S <-> ES -> E + P, the conserved quantities are:
The number of independent conservation laws in a chemical network equals:
facts.truth: n_species minus rank(S) where S is the stoichiometry matrix

facts.context: For open chemical systems with inflow/outflow of species:
facts.truth: stoichiometric conservation laws may be broken by external fluxes
distractors: no conservation laws exist in closed systems
open systems have more conservation laws

facts.context: Under mass-action kinetics, the rate of reaction A + B -> C is:
facts.truth: k × [A] × [B] where k is the rate constant

facts.context: The Lotka-Volterra predator-prey system (viewed as chemical network) has:
facts.truth: no stoichiometric conservation laws (open system with birth/death)
certain linear combinations of intermediate and product concentrations

facts.context: The deficiency of a chemical reaction network is:
facts.truth: n - l - s where n = complexes, l = linkage classes, s = rank(S)

facts.context: For glycolysis with 14 species and 12 reactions, the number of independent conservation laws is:
facts.truth: at least 2-5 depending on network structure (ATP/ADP and NAD/NADH pools)

facts.context: To verify a proposed conservation law w·c = constant numerically:
facts.truth: integrate the ODE system and check that w·c(t) has zero variance
distractors: check if w has unit norm

facts.truth: moieties or atomic groups that are rearranged but not created or destroyed
the set of all concentrations reachable from an initial condition via the network dynamics
distractors: the set of all possible initial conditions

facts.context: A non-trivial conservation law in a large network (20+ species) is typically:
facts.truth: a linear combination involving many species with non-obvious coefficients
distractors: always a sum of two species
